texto: It is more magnificent than what I thought heaven might be, and yet it is all of its wonder, as well.”... "Iris, we are shut off from it in this life because if any knew its magnificence, life itself would end, for all who are living would seek death. But as the egg must be in the nest for the bird to fly from it, so the living must live and die when nature intends so that the shell may be broken at the point when the living have wings to fly. It is as if in life we are blind, and in death we see. In life we think in error, but in death we know and love and understand.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Douglas Clegg: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “It is more magnificent than what I thought heaven might be, and yet it is all of its wonder, as w…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, heaven, living pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e termino.

"Iris, we are shut off from it in this life because if any knew its magnificence, life itself would end, for all who are living would seek death. But as the egg must be in the nest for the bird to fly from it, so the living must live and die when nature intends so that the shell may be broken at the point when the living have wings to fly. It is as if in life we are blind, and in death we see. In life we think in error, but in death we know and love and understand.

texto: We kill, kill, kill. Flesh, spirit, whatever gets in our way. It's like our whole purpose is to extinguish life. And for those who live, there's memory, like a curse. We're such a mixture of frailty and cruelty.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Douglas Clegg: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “We kill, kill, kill. Flesh, spirit, whatever gets in our way. It's like our whole purpose is to e…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, philosophy, flesh pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
